notes: each enemy should feel mechanically unique in terms of how they attack. Some more in straight lines, other more erratically or frantically around before reaching the target. Some rush in, others attack from a distance and change targets at random. Different unit types should behave differently to make them feel different.

gameRules.armorFormula: Armor provides flat damage reduction per hit. Each point of armor reduces incoming damage by 1. Minimum damage per hit is 1 (attacks can never deal 0 damage). Example: 12 damage vs 4 armor = 8 damage dealt. This makes armor very effective against fast, low-damage attacks (Autocannon, Thrasher swarms) and weak against slow, high-damage attacks (Rail Gun, Siege Walker).
gameRules.spawnSystem: Enemies spawn continuously from all 4 map edges equally. Base spawn rate starts at 1 enemy per 2 seconds per edge (4 enemies/sec total). Spawn rate increases by 5% per minute. Surges occur every ~30 seconds (+-10sec variance), spawning 8-15 enemies from a single random edge. Surge frequency accelerates by 10% per minute. Enemy composition shifts over time as new types unlock at their firstAppearance time (in seconds).
gameRules.spawnGroups: Some enemies always spawn in groups: Thrasher (3 per spawn), Blight Mite (5 per spawn), Howler (2 per spawn, always alongside other types). Group spawns count as 1 spawn event but produce multiple units.
gameRules.bossSpawns: First boss spawns at ~90 seconds (+-15sec). This earlier timing ensures players can unlock Tier 1 tech before the resource dead zone sets in. Subsequent bosses spawn every ~120 seconds, with interval decreasing by 10% each time. Bosses always spawn from a random edge with a 3-second warning indicator on the minimap and a screen-wide alert banner.
gameRules.statScaling: All enemies gain +1% HP and +1% damage per minute of game time (applied to base stats). Armor scaling: Small enemies +1 armor per 10 minutes, Large enemies +1 armor per 5 minutes, Huge enemies +2 armor per 5 minutes. Scaling is cumulative.
gameRules.populationCap: Player has a 100 unit population cap. If the cap is reached, production buildings pause their queues (resources are not spent). Queued units resume production when pop frees up. Continuous mode also pauses at cap.
gameRules.projectiles: All projectile attacks always hit their target (no miss chance). Projectile travel time is visual only for standard attacks. Arcing projectiles (Plasma Mortar, Bile Spitter, Siege Walker) have actual travel time and hit the ground location where the target was when fired -- fast enemies can partially dodge these.
gameRules.corpsePersistence: Enemy corpses remain on the battlefield for 10 seconds after death, during which Leach Towers can harvest them for Materials.
gameRules.passiveRegen: All player buildings and units regenerate 1% of max HP every 5 seconds. Enemies do not regenerate.
gameRules.ignoresBarriersExplained: When ignoresBarriers is true, the unit walks/flies through barriers without stopping or interacting with them. Flying enemies inherently ignore barriers. Ground units with ignoresBarriers clip through walls as if they aren't there (visually they may leap, phase, or bulldoze through depending on the enemy type).
gameRules.mapSize: 300x300 total grid. Player base is centered with a 120x120 buildable area (walls start as a hexagon ~100 units across, can build 10 units beyond walls). Enemies spawn from all 4 edges and must cross 90 grids of open terrain before reaching the outermost walls.
gameRules.economy: Base income: 5 Energy/sec + 5 Materials/sec. Central Tower upgrades double income (Tier 1: 10/10, Tier 2: 20/20, Tier 3: 40/40). Leach Towers and Thermal Siphons provide supplemental Materials and Energy respectively. All costs are in Energy and/or Materials. Tech Points (1 per boss kill) are earned during the run but spent only in the post-run upgrades screen.
gameRules.sellBack: All player buildings and units can be sold for 75% of total invested resources (base cost + all upgrade costs). Selling is instant with a confirmation dialog. Selling a production building mid-production refunds the building cost plus any resources spent on the in-progress unit. Queued (not yet started) units are simply removed from the queue with no cost. There is no sell hotkey -- player must select the entity and click the sell button in the info panel.
gameRules.buildGrid: Grid cells are uniform squares. Buildings snap to grid on placement with a ghost preview showing valid/invalid placement (green=valid, red=invalid). Buildings cannot overlap. 2x2 and 3x3 buildings occupy multiple grid cells. Buildings cannot be rotated. Placement requires line-of-sight to an existing player structure within 5 grids (prevents building outside the base perimeter).
gameRules.camera: Top-down perspective, slight isometric angle. WASD moves camera. Optional mouse edge-panning. Mouse scroll wheel zooms in/out. No camera rotation. Camera speed is fixed. Camera does not auto-follow selected units but can be snapped to a selected unit with a double-tap of its hotkey.
gameRules.unitSelection: Left-click to select a unit/building. Drag-box to select multiple units. Double-click a unit to select all of that type on screen. Right-click to issue move command (to ground) or attack command (on enemy). E key = attack-move (units move to target location and attack anything along the way). No control groups in initial release.
gameRules.continuousProduction: In the production queue, each slot can be set to a specific unit type. Toggling 'Continuous' on a building makes it cycle through its queued unit types indefinitely, producing each in order as resources become available. If resources are insufficient for the next unit in rotation, it skips to the next affordable one. If no units are affordable, production pauses until resources are available. Continuous mode respects the population cap.

enemies.name: Thrasher
enemies.description: Frantic swarming enemy, spawns in groups of 3 at a time (so 3x as many units per spawn as others). The bread-and-butter enemy that never stops coming. Individually weak, but in numbers they overwhelm defenses through sheer attrition. Armor is devastating against them since each claw swipe deals low damage.
enemies.visualDescription: Lean, low-slung quadruped with a feline gait. Bony exoskeleton plates along the spine, exposed sinew on the limbs. Front claws are elongated and serrated, swiping rapidly at targets. Pale grey-white skin with dark vein patterns. Eyes glow faint yellow. Moves in a frantic, skittering run with sudden directional changes.
enemies.aiBehavior: Sprints directly at the nearest target (barrier, building, or unit). Attacks whatever it reaches first. No special logic -- pure aggression. Moves in a slightly erratic zigzag pattern rather than a perfectly straight line, giving swarms a chaotic visual feel. When in a group, individual Thrashers spread slightly to different targets rather than all stacking on one.

enemies.name: Brute
enemies.description: Slow-moving frontline bruiser with punishing melee damage. Walks straight at barriers and smashes them apart. The primary wall-breaker in early game -- a few Brutes hitting the same wall section will open a gap fast. High HP and armor make them hard to drop before they reach the walls.
enemies.visualDescription: Hulking bipedal creature with a massive upper body and disproportionately long arms ending in dense, hammer-like bone growths. Thick leathery hide with overlapping chitinous plates on shoulders and chest. Dark brown-grey coloring with cracks of dull orange bioluminescence along the arms. Hunched posture, drags knuckles slightly while walking, rears back to deliver overhead smashing blows.
enemies.aiBehavior: Walks directly toward the nearest barrier or building. Attacks with slow, heavy overhead smashes that deal high damage per hit. Stays locked on its current target until it is destroyed, then moves to the next nearest. Does not chase player units -- focused entirely on structure destruction. Moves in a steady, deliberate straight line with no evasion.

enemies.name: Clugg
enemies.description: Massive living shield. Does not attack directly but has extreme HP and armor, soaking up tower fire that would otherwise kill other enemies. Ignores barriers entirely, walking through them to position itself near towers. Its huge body physically blocks projectiles aimed at enemies behind it. Emits a passive Threat Aura that forces all towers within 4 grids to target the Clugg for 3 seconds every 10 seconds (like a taunt pulse), ensuring it can't simply be ignored through targeting priority changes. Forces the player to either invest DPS in killing it or accept reduced tower efficiency while it's alive.
enemies.visualDescription: Enormous turtle-like creature with a thick, domed carapace covered in layered bone plates and calcified growths. Four stumpy, pillar-like legs support its massive bulk. No visible eyes -- navigates by vibration. The carapace has deep scoring and impact marks suggesting it has weathered immense punishment. Dull grey-brown shell with patches of dark moss-like growth. Moves with ponderous, earthquake-like steps.
enemies.aiBehavior: Walks through barriers without stopping (ignoresBarriers). Heads toward the nearest cluster of towers/buildings. Once within 3 grids of towers, stops and holds position, acting as a damage sponge. Its large hitbox (occupies 2x2 grid space visually) intercepts projectiles aimed at enemies behind it. Towers with 'Nearest' targeting will lock onto the Clugg due to proximity, wasting DPS. If all nearby towers are destroyed, slowly advances toward the next cluster. Never attacks -- purely a distraction and shield for other enemies advancing behind it.

abilities.name: Threat Aura
abilities.description: Passive. Every 10 seconds, emits a low-frequency vibration pulse (visible as a brief ground ripple expanding outward). All player towers within 4 grids are forced to target the Clugg for 3 seconds, overriding their current target priority. This ensures the Clugg cannot be completely ignored through smart targeting. Visual: ground ripple effect expanding from the Clugg, affected towers briefly flash with a yellow-orange forced-target indicator.

enemies.name: Scrit
enemies.description: Fast, erratic flying harasser. Bypasses all barriers and darts around the base attacking towers and units unpredictably. Its constant target-switching and chaotic movement make it hard for slow-firing towers to track. Not individually dangerous, but groups of Scrits create persistent harassment that distracts defenses from the main ground assault.
enemies.visualDescription: Bat-like creature with elongated, tattered wing membranes stretched between bony finger-like struts. Thin, angular body with a long barbed tail. Head is mostly mouth -- a wide gash filled with needle teeth. Dark purple-black coloring with veins of sickly green bioluminescence along the wings. Flight pattern is jerky and erratic, with sudden dives and sharp banking turns. Fires small acidic projectiles from the tail tip.
enemies.aiBehavior: Flies directly into the base ignoring all barriers. Picks a random target (tower, building, or unit) and attacks it 2-3 times, then abruptly switches to a different random target. Movement is erratic -- flies in unpredictable swooping arcs rather than straight lines, making it visually chaotic. Never hovers in one spot for long. Frequently changes altitude and direction mid-flight. If multiple Scrits are present, they do NOT coordinate -- each acts independently, spreading harassment across the base.

enemies.name: Slinker
enemies.description: Ranged heavy hitter that stays behind the front line and pelts defenses with damaging energy projectiles. High range lets it attack barriers, towers, and units from relative safety while tougher enemies absorb fire up front. A Slinker behind a Clugg is a brutal combination -- the Clugg absorbs shots while the Slinker deals steady damage. Forces the player to either push units out to engage or use long-range towers to snipe them.
enemies.visualDescription: Tall, lean bipedal creature with a hunched posture and an oversized elongated skull. The cranium splits open along the top to reveal a bioluminescent energy organ that charges and launches rapid green energy pellets. Long digitigrade legs give it a stalking gait. Mottled dark green hide with lighter green patches where energy glows through thin skin. Two vestigial arms tucked against the chest. Eyes are narrow slits glowing the same green as its projectiles.
enemies.aiBehavior: Advances toward the base but stops moving once any valid target enters its 10-grid attack range. Prefers to attack from maximum range, keeping distance from barriers. Targets the nearest visible enemy (barrier, tower, or unit). If its current target is destroyed, picks the next nearest target in range. If no targets are in range, advances slowly until one enters range, then stops again. Does not chase fleeing units -- holds position and picks a new target. Tends to cluster behind heavier enemies (Brutes, Cluggs) using them as cover while firing over/around them.

enemies.name: Polus
enemies.description: Agile barrier-hopper that uses its Leap ability to vault over walls and land inside the base. Once inside, it targets player units with rapid spine volleys. Unlike flying enemies that casually bypass barriers, the Polus must actively leap over them (3 sec cooldown), creating windows where it can be caught outside walls. Fast and annoying but fragile -- a few tower shots will kill it. Dangerous in numbers when they leap in simultaneously.
enemies.visualDescription: Compact four-legged creature with powerful spring-loaded hind legs built for explosive jumping. Sleek black exoskeleton with crimson markings along the spine and joints. Back is covered in rows of detachable bone spines that it launches as projectiles. Narrow, eyeless head with sensory pits. Crouches low before leaping, launching itself in a high arc over obstacles. Lands with claws splayed for grip on any surface.
enemies.aiBehavior: Runs toward the base walls at high speed. When it reaches within 1 grid of a barrier or obstruction, uses its Leap ability to vault over it (if Leap is off cooldown). After landing inside the base, prioritizes attacking player units over buildings. Fires spine projectiles at range 5 while moving, allowing it to kite and harass. Cannot walk through occupied grid spaces -- must leap over them. If Leap is on cooldown and blocked by a barrier, will attack the barrier until Leap is ready again. Multiple Polus arriving together can overwhelm by leaping in from different wall sections simultaneously.

abilities.name: Leap
abilities.description: When within 1 grid of any barrier or obstruction, the Polus launches itself in a high arc over the obstacle, landing up to 4 grids away on the other side. 3-second cooldown between leaps. The Polus can land on occupied grid spaces temporarily (perched on top of structures) to attack from an elevated position. Cannot walk through occupied spaces normally -- must leap over them. Visual: crouches briefly, then springs into a visible arc, landing with a sharp impact.

enemies.name: Terror Bringer
enemies.description: The first boss enemy. A massive, terrifyingly fast brute that charges in a straight line toward the Central Tower, smashing through any barriers or buildings in its path. Does not deviate or chase other targets -- single-minded in its rush to destroy the Central Tower. Its Death Blast on death deals catastrophic AoE damage, punishing players who kill it near critical infrastructure. The player must either kill it far from the base or accept the collateral damage.
enemies.visualDescription: Towering bipedal monstrosity standing 3x human height, built like a living battering ram. Massive armored skull with a thick bone crest designed for ramming impacts. Legs are trunk-like with clawed feet that tear the ground as it sprints. Upper body leans forward in a perpetual charge stance. Hide is covered in thick overlapping plates of dark chitinous armor, scarred and cracked from previous battles. Dull red bioluminescence pulses from gaps between armor plates, intensifying as it takes damage. When it dies, the red glow reaches blinding intensity before detonating.
enemies.aiBehavior: Charges in a straight line from its spawn point directly toward the Central Tower. Does NOT pathfind -- moves in a literal straight line. Anything in its path (barriers, towers, units) takes full melee damage as the Terror Bringer rams through it. It attacks barriers/buildings in its path at 2 attacks/sec while pushing forward, destroying them to continue its charge. Ignores all enemies and structures not directly in its charge path. Splash damage (1 grid radius, 50%) hits things adjacent to its path. If its direct path is blocked by high-HP structures, it stops and pounds through them before continuing. Never deviates to chase units. On death, triggers Death Blast after a 2-second visual wind-up (red glow intensifies, body swells) giving nearby units a brief window to retreat.

abilities.name: Death Blast
abilities.description: On death, enters a 2-second detonation wind-up (body glows bright red, swells visibly, emits rising audio tone) before exploding for 500 damage in a 3 grid radius. The 2-second delay gives mobile units a window to retreat, but stationary buildings cannot escape. Deals 50% reduced damage to buildings (250 effective vs structures) to prevent total base wipes. Still devastating to units and clusters of enemies caught in the blast.

enemies.name: Blight Mite
enemies.description: Suicide swarm unit. Sprints toward barriers and buildings and detonates on contact. Spawns in groups of 5. Designed to punish heavy walling strategies by blowing holes in defenses for other enemies to pour through.
enemies.visualDescription: Tiny insect-like body barely larger than a boot, supported by 6 spindly skittering legs that move in a frantic blur. A swollen, translucent volatile sac sits on its back, filled with glowing sickly green fluid that pulses faster as the Mite gets closer to its target -- slow throbs at range, rapid flickering when near detonation. Dark purple chitinous body with green bioluminescent veins. No visible head -- just a forward-facing cluster of sensory nubs. When it detonates, the sac ruptures in a bright green-yellow flash expanding outward, leaving a brief acid splash on the ground.
enemies.aiBehavior: Sprints in a direct line toward the nearest barrier or building. Does not attack normally -- detonates on contact with any structure, dealing its explosion damage. If no barriers remain, targets the nearest building instead. Erratic jittery movement as it runs, zigzagging slightly.

abilities.name: Self-Destruct
abilities.description: Detonates on contact with a barrier or building, dealing 80 damage in a 2 grid radius. Destroys self. Damage hits all friendly and enemy units/structures in the blast zone.

enemies.name: Gorger
enemies.description: Unit-hunter bruiser. Ignores buildings entirely and charges straight at player units to tear them apart. Faster than the Brute, with a frenzy mechanic that makes it more dangerous the closer it is to death. Forces the player to micro units away or commit to killing it fast.
enemies.visualDescription: Hunched, powerfully built quadruped with an oversized jaw that can unhinge for devastating bites. Long serrated forelimbs with blade-like claws drag forward as it prowls. Mottled red-brown hide stretched tight over bulging muscle, with exposed yellowish bone ridges running along the spine and shoulders. Small, predatory eyes glow a deep red. Moves with a low, stalking lope that shifts to explosive sprinting when it spots prey. In Frenzy state (below 30% HP): entire body pulses with a deep red glow emanating from beneath the skin, movements become jerky and hyper-aggressive, jaw hangs open with visible saliva, bone ridges glow hot orange.
enemies.aiBehavior: Completely ignores barriers and buildings -- phases through walls as if they aren't there (visually: briefly becomes semi-transparent when passing through structures). Charges directly at the nearest player unit. Will chase a target relentlessly, switching only if its current target dies or moves out of the base area. When below 30% HP, enters Frenzy state -- visually glows red, movement becomes erratic and more aggressive, attack speed and move speed increase.

abilities.name: Frenzy
abilities.description: When HP drops below 30%, gains +50% attack speed and +25% move speed. Visual: body glows red, movements become more erratic and aggressive.

enemies.name: Gloom Wing
enemies.description: Heavy flying bomber. Unlike the fast chaotic Scrit, the Gloom Wing is a slow, deliberate flyer that drops devastating AoE blasts onto clusters of towers. Forces the player to spread out their tower placement or invest in anti-air. A high-priority target when it appears.
enemies.visualDescription: Massive manta ray-like flyer with a wide dark wingspan stretching 3-4 grid units across. Flat, triangular body with a heavy underbelly lined with pulsing bioluminescent sacs that swell and release downward in bombing attacks. Deep black and midnight blue coloring with faint cyan-white bioluminescent veins tracing the wing edges and spine. No visible head -- sensory organs are embedded along the leading wing edge. Tail is a long, trailing tendril. Flight animation is slow and deliberate with smooth, sweeping wing beats. Banking turns are wide and graceful. When attacking, the underbelly sacs glow bright before rupturing downward with a visible falling projectile that detonates on impact with a blue-white flash.
enemies.aiBehavior: Flies in slow sweeping passes over the base, hovering above clusters of towers. Drops AoE blasts from above, preferring to target areas with the highest density of buildings. Moves in wide arcs rather than straight lines, circling the base before committing to an attack run.

abilities.name: Carpet Bomb
abilities.description: Each attack drops a blast that hits all structures and units in a 2 grid radius for 60% splash damage. Prefers targeting clusters of 2+ towers within its splash range.

enemies.name: Bile Spitter
enemies.description: Ultra long-range siege attacker. Hangs far back behind the front line and lobs corrosive projectiles deep into the base, targeting towers and production buildings. Very slow moving and slow firing, but each hit strips armor and deals AoE. The kind of enemy that forces the player to send units out to deal with it.
enemies.visualDescription: bloated slug-like body with a grotesquely swollen head that rears back and spits arcing globs of acid, pale yellowish skin with dark veins, 4 stubby legs barely supporting its mass
enemies.aiBehavior: Stops moving as soon as any tower or building is within its 18 range and begins lobbing arcing projectiles over walls. Prioritizes towers and production buildings deep in the base over frontline barriers. Will reposition slowly if its current targets are destroyed. Never approaches closer than range 12 if possible.

abilities.name: Corrosive Impact
abilities.description: Each hit reduces the target's armor by 1 for 10 seconds (stacks up to 3 times). The acid pool left by the projectile persists for 3 seconds, dealing 5 damage per second to anything standing in it.

enemies.name: Howler
enemies.description: Support swarm that buffs all nearby enemies. Weak in combat but massively amplifies the threat of everything around it. Spawns in pairs mixed into groups of other enemies. A high-priority kill target -- experienced players will learn to snipe these first before dealing with the buffed wave around them.
enemies.visualDescription: Small, hunched bipedal creature with a grotesquely oversized cranium that makes up nearly half its body mass. The skull splits open along a horizontal seam to emit its War Cry -- revealing a pulsing red-pink organ inside that vibrates to produce the buff wave. Translucent, almost albino pale skin through which dark internal organs and vein networks are visually visible. Twitchy, nervous movements with head constantly jerking side to side. Thin, spindly limbs with bony joints. Eyes are large, milky, and unfocused. When emitting War Cry: skull splits open, visible red pulse wave expands outward every 2 seconds as concentric rings on the ground, nearby buffed enemies gain a faint red glow outline.
enemies.aiBehavior: Stays in the middle of enemy groups rather than rushing to the front. Moves at the pace of nearby allies rather than sprinting ahead. Continuously emits its War Cry buff aura. If it finds itself alone with no allies in range, it flees toward the nearest group of enemies rather than attacking solo. Spawns in pairs, always alongside other enemy types.

abilities.name: War Cry
abilities.description: Passive aura. All enemies within 4 grid units gain +20% damage and +15% move speed while the Howler is alive. Effect does not stack with multiple Howlers. Visual: visible pulse wave radiating outward every 2 seconds, buffed enemies have a faint red glow.
